old_title: Iran war live: Amazon data centre hit by cruise missiles as Tehran steps up attacks
new_title: Iran war latest: Trump threatens to attack nuclear site as cost of war revealed
old_text:
Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they targeted infrastructure belonging to US company Amazon AMZN.O in Bahrain, according to a statements published by state media on Tuesday.
There was no official confirmation from Bahrain and Reuters could not independently verify the incident. Iran's semi-official Tasnim news agency said that the facility was part of the company's central data infrastructure.
US president Donald Trump has vowed to make Iran pay "many times over" for the deaths of US soldiers as his troops launched the 10th consecutive night of attacks.
Trump issued the warning on Monday after he claimed the US had hit Iran "very hard" in retaliation for the deaths of three American soldiers in Jordan and Iraq.
This morning the Iranian army claimed to have targeted three major US military installations in Kuwait using attack drones. Iran on Monday had vowed to continue striking American targets "for years" if required as diplomacy slipped further out of view.
Iran's Revolutionary Guard also pushed back on Trump's recent claims that Iran is running out of munitions, as the regime continues to pound US facilities in neighbouring countries.
"[Trump] should know that, by God's grace, even if this war lasts for several years, until the very last day, our missiles and drones will rain down on American criminals," the statement said.
Reza Pahlavi says he received DMs from IRGC members wanting to 'break away'
In an interview with DRM News, Reza Pahlavi has said that he receives social media messages from members of the Iranian establishment that are hoping to break away from it.
"I began receiving some DMs in my social media by elements that say, you know, 'I'm a basij in Mashhad,' or 'I'm a member of the IRGC in such and such country. We hate this regime. We're not with them. We're looking for an opportunity to break away'," he said.
"It's not that I'm in fact talking to the IRGC command - not at all. But there were elements of the Sepah, of course the military as well, that would communicate with me discreetly."
Pentagon withheld information on dozens of US military injuries in Iran war: report
The Pentagon withheld information on dozens of military injuries suffered by US forces during the war in Iran, according to a report.
At least two service members were killed and one missing after Iranian attacks on the Muwaffaq Salti US Air Base in Jordan on Friday.
They were identified on Monday as 25-year-old 1st Lt Tyler James Feehan from Ewa Beach, Hawaii and 19-year-old Pvt. Isabella Gonzales from Carrolton, Texas.
But in the week leading up to the incident, Iran carried out three other attacks against the US in Jordan that injured dozens of service members and damaged several helicopters, US officials told the New York Times.
The Pentagon did not disclose the attacks nor release details of the casualties and damage inflicted.
Oil prices remain close to one-month high as Yemen announces Red Sea blockade
Oil prices held close to a one-month high on Tuesday despite efforts to broker a truce between the US and Iran as the widening conflict and a new threat to Red Sea shipping kept supply fears alive.
Brent crude, the international benchmark, slipped 0.4 per cent to $88.87 a barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate held steady at $82.47. Both remained near the highs reached the day before, when Brent broke above $90 for the first time in more than a month.
The slight retreat came after reports of a mediation effort to pause the fighting between US and Iran. Tehran had received a proposal from mediators for a 10-day ceasefire, an effort to salvage an interim deal signed on 17 June to end the US-Israeli war on Iran that started on 28 February, a senior Iranian official told Reuters.
Iran's Guard target Amazon infrastructure in Bahrain
Iran's Revolutionary Guard have claimed to have targeted US retail giant Amazon's infrastructure in Bahrain.
The Guard have also launched attacks on US bases in Jordan and Kuwait.
Iran claims strikes on US bases in Jordan
Iran's Revolutionary Guard this morning claimed it launched an attack on US military bases in Jordan.
The Guard claimed it destroyed a missile defence radar system and an F-15 aircraft inside a shelter during the attack.
Pentagon chief says 100 US troopers injured in attack
The Pentagon's chief spokesperson said nearly 100 US service members have been injured since the US restarted strikes on 7 July, and 96 per cent of them have returned to duty.
"The vast majority of injuries experienced were minor concussions," Sean Parnell posted on X in response to a New York Times report that the Pentagon has withheld information about troop injuries from Iranian strikes. He denied that the Pentagon was hiding data about injuries.
However, the Defense Casualty Analysis System, the military's clearinghouse for reporting deaths and injuries in conflict, has not been updated as of last night with the latest attacks.
Yemen's Houthis declare fresh naval blockade in the Middle East
Yemeni rebel group, the Houthis, have declared a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, a move that would mark a major escalation in conflict in the region and further disrupt global energy supplies beyond the Gulf.
In a statement, the Iran-aligned armed group said that they were declaring "a maritime embargo against the criminal Saudi enemy, based on the equation of 'an eye for an eye', effective immediately".
They said the decision was in response to what they described as "an unjust and oppressive siege" imposed on Yemen by the Saudis.
More here.
Israeli strikes kill five in Gaza
At least five Palestinians were killed and several others injured in Israeli airstrikes across Gaza.
A man and his daughter were killed when an Israeli drone struck the Al-Yaman Al-Saeed shelter for displaced people in the Jabalia refugee camp in northern Gaza, Anadolu reported.
Three others were earlier killed when another Israeli drone struck a vehicle belonging to the Desert Sands Insurance Company near the British Field Hospital in the Al-Mawasi area of Khan Younis, according to reports.
Explosions heard in western Iranian city
Several explosions were heard during the early hours of the day in the western Iranian city of Abdanan, governor Behzad Nourmohammadi said.
"Fortunately, this incident did not result in any casualties, and the responsible agencies are investigating the dimensions of the issue," he said.
Oil prices remain close to one-month high as Yemen announces Red Sea blockade
Oil prices held close to a one-month high on Tuesday despite efforts to broker a truce between the US and Iran as the widening conflict and a new threat to Red Sea shipping kept supply fears alive.
Brent crude, the international benchmark, slipped 0.4 per cent to $88.87 a barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate held steady at $82.47. Both remained near the highs reached the day before, when Brent broke above $90 for the first time in more than a month.
The slight retreat came after reports of a mediation effort to pause the fighting between US and Iran. Tehran had received a proposal from mediators for a 10-day ceasefire, an effort to salvage an interim deal signed on 17 June to end the US-Israeli war on Iran that started on 28 February, a senior Iranian official told Reuters.
More here.
new_text:
Donald Trump has threatened to renew strikes on Iran's heavily fortified "Pickaxe Mountain" nuclear site, as the staggering human and financial toll of the five-month war between the US and Iran came into focus.
Trump said the US would target the Pickaxe Mountain area, located near Iran's heavily damaged Natanz uranium enrichment facility, "pretty soon."
The site - known in Iran as Kuh-e Kolang Gaz La - hosts two deeply buried tunnel complexes that experts assess as beyond the reach of the most powerful bunker buster bombs in the US arsenal.
Appearing at the Senate on Tuesday, Trump's defence secretary Pete Hegseth unraveled over questions about the growing cost of the Trump administration's war in Iran, estimating the financial toll has now reached $37.5bn. At the same time, he suggested Trump should be praised for starting the war.
Hegseth is asking for another $67 billion from Congress to cover mounting costs for the war, among other demands.
In terms of personnel costs, US officials say at least 18 American troops have died since the beginning of the war, and more than 400 others have been injured.
French diplomats not ill-treated by Iranian personnel, says Tehran
Two French diplomats at the centre of a dispute between France and Iran were not ill-treated by Iranian security personnel, according to an Iranian foreign ministry official quoted by state media on Wednesday.
France has said that the diplomats in Tehran had their phones taken, were forbidden to contact the embassy and verbally intimidated, breaking international diplomatic conventions. One of the officials, the cultural attache, was hit.
The French foreign ministry on Tuesday summoned the Iranian charge d'affaires to protest the incident.
"France expects the Iranian authorities to shed light on this incident, punish the perpetrators, and ensure the security of its premises and personnel, in accordance with their international obligations," the French foreign ministry said on Tuesday.
According to Iranian Foreign Ministry official Mohammad Tanhaei, Iranian security personnel questioned two French embassy staff who held a meeting with individuals who are being investigated for security reasons.
"Security agents transferred the two individuals to the diplomatic police as soon as they realised their link to the French embassy," he was quoted as saying, adding that there was no intimidation.
France's foreign ministry did not immediately respond to a request for comment on Wednesday.
Watch: Trump warns Iran the US 'has been nice' so far in attacks as threat of full-blown war grows
Pakistan seeks $10 billion fund from US after mediating Iran talks: report
Pakistan has asked the United States for a $10 billion exchange stabilization facility, according to a source briefed on the matter, which, if approved, could provide a lifeline for the cash-strapped South Asian economy.
The request, which is being reported for the first time, follows Pakistan's role in brokering talks over the Iran war, which raised its diplomatic profile and stirred hopes that it could seek economic gains from Washington and other partners.
In the request to U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, Islamabad is seeking a Bilateral Exchange Stabilization Support Facility between the U.S. and the Pakistani government worth $10 billion with maturity of up to five years.
The facility, if agreed to, would bolster Pakistan's reserves, ease pressure on the rupee and reduce its reliance on multilateral financing, even as Islamabad undertakes tighter fiscal and monetary policies in line with its International Monetary Fund program.
The U.S. Treasury declined to comment on the reported request.
Iran executes man over January protests, judiciary news agency says
Iran executed a man for charges linked to protests in the January, the judiciary's Mizan news agency reported on Wednesday.
Mizan identified the man as Mehdi Khaneki and said he was executed for "operational actions" that benefited Israel, the United States and hostile groups. It said he also produced and possessed weapons.
Iran renews strikes on Jordan and Bahrain
Iran's military is claiming fresh strikes on US military targets in Jordan and Bahrain, per state media.
Targets were identified in Jordan's Al Azraq base and Bahrain's Sheikh Isa air base.
Mapped: Traffic still piled up outside Strait of Hormuz
Traffic around the Strait of Hormuz was still subdued on Wednesday as clashes between the US and Iran continue to escalate.
Ship tracking data from Marine Traffic shows limited movement around the Strait itself, which has been affected by spoofing and radio interference since the start of the conflict.
Rubio discusses Iran war with Jaishankar ahead talks with Chinese diplomat
Marco Rubio held talks with Indian foreign minister S. Jaishankar on the sidelines of the Asean meeting, discussing the Iran war as he is met counterparts of the Quad grouping.
Rubio reaffirmed the commitment of Quad - the US, India, Australia and Japan - to maritime security in the Indo-Pacific.
"We are united in our conviction that peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific maritime domain underpin the security and prosperity of the region," the leaders said in a statement.
Rubio added in a post on X that, "The Quad remains a priority, and we will meet again later this year."
After his talks with Jaishankar, he said an interim US-India trade deal that Washington said is "almost complete."
"The world is extremely turbulent, and it is increasingly evident that no single nation or even grouping can deal with it on its own," Jaishankar said.
Rubio is also expected to meet Chinese foreign minister Wang Yi on the sidelines of the summit.
Iran's demands over the Strait of Hormuz would set a dangerous precedent, Rubio tells Asean leaders
US secretary of state Marco Rubio has warned that Iran's demand to control and levy tolls on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz would endanger the global economy and set a dangerous precedent for international waterways.
Speaking at an Asean foreign ministers' meeting in Manila on Wednesday, Rubio said allowing a country to control an international shipping lane and attack vessels that refuse to pay would undermine the principle of freedom of navigation.
"If we create a precedent in the Middle East where a nation state can decide that they are going to control an international waterway, charge a toll and if you don't pay them, blow up your ships, we have created a very dangerous precedent which will repeat itself in other parts of the world, including in this region," he said.
Trump threatens to strike Iran's Pickaxe Mountain nuclear site
Donald Trump has warned that the US military will hit Iran's "Pickaxe Mountain" nuclear complex soon.
Trump said the US military would be "hitting that area pretty soon, and very heavily," referring to Pickaxe Mountain - known in Iran as Kuh-e Kolang Gaz La - a fortified underground site near one of the country's main nuclear enrichment facilities.
Pickaxe Mountain, about 220km south of Tehran and near the Natanz nuclear facility, is believed to house an underground complex buried nearly 100 metres inside the mountain.
Little is known about the site, which experts say is still under construction, and is likely too deep to be reached by even the US's most advanced bunker buster missiles.
According to the Institute for Science and International Security (ISIS), the facility was not targeted during the US-Israeli strikes in either the 2025 or 2026 conflicts.
